overview: This four-color Legacy deck leverages card advantage and versatile removal to control the pace of the game. By cycling through the library with Brainstorm and efficiently neutralizing threats with Swords to Plowshares, it creates openings for tempo engines like Orcish Bowmasters. The goal is to maintain board presence while outmaneuvering opponents through superior resource management.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through sustained card advantage and removal
Closing games with Orcish Bowmasters and its generated tokens
Clearing the board with Swords to Plowshares to deploy unblocked threats

keep_criteria: Hands containing early Swords to Plowshares or Brainstorm
Mana bases that support two-color starts
Low-cost threats or interaction that can be played on turn one or two

Keep hands with 2–3 lands (prioritizing fetch/shock or duals that can produce WUBG), a turn-1 play (land, Orcish Bowmasters, or Delver), and at least one disruption spell (Thoughtseize, Brainstorm, or a counterspell).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any interaction or cantrips
Mana screws with four or more lands
Slow hands reliant on late-game cards without early answers
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, hands with only removal and no threats/land, or hands missing colored sources for your key spells.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and casting Brainstorm to find answers
Hold Swords to Plowshares for opponent's early threats
Deploy Orcish Bowmasters on curve to establish tempo
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Orcish Bowmasters or Delver of Secrets, or cast Thoughtseize on a key threat.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast Brainstorm to filter into threats or answers, or play a second land and cast a delve creature (Tarmogoyf, Cryptbreaker).
Sequence lands to ensure you can produce the necessary colors for removal and delve costs while maintaining a steady curve.
mid_game: Use Swords to Plowshares to clear the way for your threats
Leverage Bowmasters' triggered ability to generate card advantage
Maintain a balance of lands and spells to avoid being stuck
Use Brainstorm to filter into threats or counterspells based on board state.
Manage your graveyard carefully—delve creatures want to be cast from it, but avoid overcommitting to it if the opponent has graveyard hate.
Use Liliana of the Veil or Dark Confidant to maintain card advantage while monitoring your life total.
Commit threats when you have removal or counterspells to protect them and the opponent has no sweepers.
Hold back if they have untapped mana for counters or a known sweeper in hand.
Use counterspells on game-winning threats or key combo pieces rather than cheap spells.
late_game: Stabilize with remaining removal and card draw
Race with unblocked threats while managing life totals
Use Swords to Plowshares for life gain to outlast attrition wars
Cryptbreaker, Tarmogoyf, and Delver of Secrets (Inferno Titan/Insectile Aberration).
Win by establishing board presence, using delve to cast them cheaply, and protecting them with counterspells or removal.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, removal for their threats, and they have no sweepers or graveyard hate in play.
Commit when you can close the game in 1–2 turns or when you’ve forced them to use their answers on multiple threats.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.role: Cantrip for card advantage and consistency
key_cards.notes: Use to dig for removal or threats; return three cards to manage hand size and set up future turns

key_cards.name: Swords to Plowshares
key_cards.notes: Exile or exile opponent's key threats; use the exile mode against indestructible or hexproof creatures when possible

key_cards.name: Orcish Bowmasters
key_cards.role: Tempo threat and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to force trades; use the token generation to block or push damage while drawing a card

sequencing_tips: Hold Swords to Plowshares until you see the opponent's key threats
Cast Brainstorm after assessing the board to maximize hand optimization
Play Orcish Bowmasters early to establish board presence before opponent stabilizes
Manage life totals carefully when using Swords to Plowshares for removal
Delve creatures are vulnerable to graveyard hate
always have a plan B like direct damage or early pressure.
Brainstorm is crucial for consistency—use it to find answers or threats based on board state.
Orcish Bowmasters is a fragile but effective early threat
protect it or use it to trade into their removal.
Avoid overextending into sweepers and always track the opponent’s graveyard to anticipate delve threats or recursion.

matchups.advice: Hold Swords to Plowshares for their biggest threats and play Orcish Bowmasters early to disrupt their rhythm.
Race with Orcish Bowmasters and use Swords to Plowshares to clear their counterspells or threats.
Disrupt their combo pieces with Swords to Plowshares and race with Orcish Bowmasters before they assemble.

Use Brainstorm to find cheaper threats or counterspells. Cast spells in response to their counters or when they’re tapped out. Use discard to remove their counterspells, and prioritize casting threats when they have no mana to respond. Delve doesn’t bypass counterspells, so timing and card advantage are key.

Apply early pressure to race them. Use removal on their mana rocks and early threats. If they get ahead, use counterspells on their win conditions and stabilize with removal. Don’t let them establish a massive mana base; disrupt their acceleration early.

Win on card advantage and better sequencing. Use discard to remove their counterspells and key threats. Hold up counterspells for their key plays and prioritize graveyard advantage. Track their hand and adjust your timing to outmaneuver them in the late game.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate, sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or hold up mana for counterspells.
Track their graveyard size to anticipate delve threats or recursion.
